purpose: Current-phase task checklist generated from the agent-maintained source coverage matrix and platform capability check. Use it before editing and update the matrix/check after work.
instructions: Start with implementationGroups as the current phase implementation contract: implement each group, then use mustComplete entries as exact row-level checks.

Use planContract as upstream planning ownership evidence; resolve its alignmentFindings by updating plannedPhase/matrix/platform evidence or by documenting sourceOverride/analysisGap when source inspection disagrees.
Keep grouped business behavior discoverable through precise target endpoints; avoid closing unrelated features only through root/shell/whole-file endpoints.
Merge source files only when it improves target clarity and still preserves per-feature evidence, tests, and controllable file size.
Treat mustComplete entries as the current phase's executable row-level task list.
Use behaviorAudit as the current phase behavior audit; it checks closed critical/high rows for source behavior, target behavior, parity, and verification evidence.

Use uiContract entries as static-analysis planning aids, not complete UI specifications: preserve listed source-visible screen structure, route/back behavior, actions, state feedback, and visual evidence needs, then re-read source/resources/navigation for UI behavior the static analysis may have missed.
Do not close the phase while current-phase critical/high entries are open, weakly deferred, or missing evidence.
For every closed critical/high source feature, record sourceBehavior, targetBehavior, parityLevel, targetEndpoints, and verificationEvidence in source-coverage-matrix.json.
A build pass alone is not per-feature verification; cite tests, manual/emulator evidence, or explicit verificationEvidence for behavior closure.
Use implemented-simplified only with concrete simplification reason, preserved-behavior explanation, target endpoints or tests, and source/platform evidence.

If a suitable HarmonyOS/Cangjie API, Kit, example, or package exists for a platform capability, use it or record a platform replacement; do not close the item with local mock data, sample data, in-memory simulation, or another fallback.
Use deferred only for concrete blockers; otherwise implement faithfully or platform-replace with evidence.
Update source-coverage-matrix.json/md and platform-capability-check.json/md before phase completion.
If this checklist is empty but the phase has real scope in plan.md/plan.json, assign plannedPhase values first.

requiredTargetCapabilitySearch: HarmonyOS Cangjie media library album query
HarmonyOS media assets photo album Cangjie API
HarmonyOS Examples media library photo picker Cangjie
targetCapabilitySearch: Phase-04 source inspection found no Android media-store, album, gallery picker, content URI, or runtime media permission flow in the home/history implementation surface. A repository-wide grep over D:\Kotlin2Cangjie\Foodike\app\src\main\java found no MediaStore, contentResolver, ACTION_PICK, GetContent, OpenDocument, PickVisualMedia, rememberLauncherForActivityResult, READ_EXTERNAL_STORAGE, or READ_MEDIA* usage.

The actual phase-04 UI code renders bundled app resources through painterResource and R.drawable only, including presentation/history/components/HistorySection.kt:111-115 and 240-243, presentation/components/RestaurantCard.kt:70-76 and 88-89, and the static catalog media ids in data/data_source/FakeData.kt:512-803.

Current HarmonyOS Cangjie SDK does provide a direct media-library and album API through ohos.file.photo_access_helper, including getPhotoAccessHelper(context: UIAbilityContext): PhotoAccessHelper and PhotoAccessHelper.getAlbums(...), so this row is resolved as a false-positive phase match rather than missing platform support.
Phase-04 implementation confirmed the translated home/history batch still uses packaged resource images only; no additional user-media or album flow was introduced while closing the scoped rows.

Focused phase-04 verification on 2026-06-28 reran a repository-wide grep across `D:\Kotlin2Cangjie\Foodike\app\src\main\java` for `MediaStore|photo|gallery|picker|READ_MEDIA|READ_EXTERNAL_STORAGE|ContentResolver|Uri|Exif|zoom` and found no matches, reinforcing that the current home/history scope has no real user-media or album-discovery flow.

Focused target verification on 2026-06-28 ran `cjpm test --target aarch64-linux-ohos` from `entry/src/test/cangjie`; `phase04_home_history_discovery_test.cj` passed while home/history surfaces continued to rely on packaged media endpoints only.
platformDeferredOrFallback.targetApiOrLibrary: HarmonyOS Cangjie MediaLibraryKit / ohos.file.photo_access_helper (getPhotoAccessHelper, PhotoAccessHelper.getAlbums, album/media asset APIs) exists in the current SDK, but phase-04 home/history source behavior only consumes bundled drawable resources and does not browse user albums.
platformDeferredOrFallback.targetStrategy: Treat the phase-04 media-library requirement as a source-analysis false positive. Implement home/history images from packaged app media resources only; do not add album discovery, gallery picker, or media permission scaffolding in this phase unless later source-backed behavior proves a real user-media flow.

implementationGroups.title: Architecture risk: broad target endpoints
requiredBehaviors: `buildHomeRoute` refreshes the repository-backed home state on each route render so the feed sections reflect current liked/search/filter state whenever the route is entered or updated.
`FoodikeBootstrap` stores home query/filter route state and `HomeStore.buildFilteredState(...)` rebuilds ads, categories, favourites, and restaurants from current repositories on each render.
`HomeStore` mirrors the state aggregation without any permission flow.
`buildHomeRoute` recomputes `HomeStore.buildFilteredState(...)` from repository data when the home route renders, preserving entry-time feed loading.

`HomeStore.loadAdvertisements()`, `loadRecommendedCategories()`, `loadFavouriteRestaurants()`, and `loadRestaurantCatalog()` now name the translated repository-backed state slices explicitly, while `buildVisibleState(...)` and `buildFilteredState(...)` pres...
`renderSearchBar` and `usesLocalSearchState()` keep only route-local query state without any preference persistence.

`renderSearchBar` feeds route-level query state into `HomeStore.buildFilteredState(...)` and `HistoryStore.buildVisibleState(...)`, enabling immediate home/history filtering from the shared search surface.
`buildHistoryRoute` reloads `HistoryStore.buildVisibleState(...)` on each route render so the current liked-restaurant state is reflected whenever the page is entered or updated.
`HistoryStore` plus `FoodikeBootstrap` route state expose observable search/tab state and reload the repository-backed favourites list on each rebuild.
`bootstrap_page.cj` keeps `homeSelectedFilter` in route state, and `buildFilterRow` re-renders selected styling while `HomeStore.buildFilteredState(...)` applies the active chip to visible lists.
`FoodikeBootstrap::buildHistoryRoute` binds current query/tab state and recomputes `HistoryStore.buildVisibleState(...)` whenever the history route is shown.
`HomeStore::toggleFilter(...)` and `matchesTag(...)` preserve the source chip-selection semantics by resolving the tapped filter string into the corresponding visible-list predicate.
`HomePageState` remains a pure translated state container with no permission path.
`HistoryPageState` and the route-local history state fields keep equivalent plain state for favourites, records, query, and tab selection without any permission handling.
